heading: Overview
body:
[Collections](manual/reference/glossary) are groupings of MongoDB documents,
similar to an RDBMS (Relational Database Management System)
table. A collection exists within a single database.

Even if a collection doesn't contain any documents, it still comes with
a resource cost because you can't drop the [default \_id index](manual/indexes). For small collections, this index doesn't
take up much space. If you have thousands of collections, these indexes
can add up in resources and strain your database allocation.

If your deployment contains unnecessary or a large number of collections
that keeps increasing, to reduce the resource requirements of your application,
restructure your data to remove unnecessary collections.
